text: Does Strang-splitting B-NLS (Madelung-Psi for NLS sector + RK4/upwind for u equation) preserve Mcs? Does ||m(t)||_2 grow numerically? What is the natural method coupling between the two sub-systems?

method: Method A (on-manifold Madelung-Psi Strang: boost(dt/2)->kin(dt/2)->nl(dt)->kin(dt/2)->boost(dt/2); u := Im(conj(Psi) d_x Psi)) vs Method B (decoupled Strang: half upwind-Burgers for u + Madelung-Psi NLS + half upwind-Burgers; NO boost coupling) vs Method C (decoupled Lie: full upwind-Burgers then full Madelung-Psi).

rationale: Method A is on-manifold by construction: u := Im(conj(Psi) Psi_x) so m = u - N*phi_x is zero to machine precision (~1e-13 ||.||_2). Methods B and C decouple u from (N,phi) and let ||m|| grow to ~0.21 within T=6 (~0% of ||u||_2 of order 0.5*sqrt(L)=2.7, so ~8% relative). The soliton peak drifts 3.16 in Method A (matching local u_peak ~ 0.5 transport) but only 1.5 in B/C (only the bare phi_x=0.25 transport, because the decoupled u-Burgers shocks and the NLS sub-step drops the boost). Mass conservation is excellent in all three (1e-12) thanks to mass-preserving NLS split-step.

text: Method A wins decisively on Mcs preservation. Propose E2: stress-test Method A by trying alternative coupling orders (Lie split: full boost -> full NLS) and adding a parameter sweep dt=0.005 to find dt boundary. Also include a comparison-of-orderings run.

method: Method A variants: (A-strang) Strang as in E1, (A-lie) Lie split [boost(dt) -> kinetic(dt) -> nonlinear(dt)], at dt in {0.005, 0.001} to find the dt frontier. Method D added: full coupled RK4 on (u, N, phi) with quantum-potential regularization eps=1e-8 (no operator split).

A_strang_dt0.005.comment: Mcs preserved (u := Im(conj Psi Psi_x) by construction) but dynamics unstable: kinetic step aliases at dt > ~0.003

A_lie_dt0.001.comment: Lie ordering matches Strang here -- IC sits on Mcs so the boost sub-step is essentially commutative with the other two.

D_RK4_dt0.0005.reason: stiff quantum potential 1/sqrt(N) in soliton tail blows up RK4 with no operator splitting

rationale: Strong knowledge: Mcs is preserved to MACHINE PRECISION only when u is reconstructed from Psi each step (u := Im(conj(Psi) Psi_x)) -- i.e., when the manifold is built into the state representation. Independent advancement of u (no matter the ordering or sub-stepping detail) lets ||m|| drift linearly. Ordering (Strang vs Lie) inside Method A is essentially equivalent for an on-Mcs IC. Coupled RK4 on the original 3-variable form is unusable due to QP stiffness. dt threshold for the on-manifold split: dt <= 0.001 stable, dt = 0.005 unstable (aliasing in kinetic propagator). Energy drifts ~8e-2 over T=6 (~0.5% of |E|), which is expected for second-order Strang.

text: STOP. Knowledge sufficient. Method A (on-manifold Madelung-Psi Strang split, with u reconstructed from Psi) is the canonical B-NLS integrator on Mcs. Record: Mcs preservation is a *representation* property, not a *time-stepping* property -- it requires that u stay algebraically tied to Psi, not that the time integrator be high-order. Write knowledge_findings.json and reasoning.md.
